panels.title: Overall Accuracy Rate

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(correct_predictions)*100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(total_predictions),0), 1) as value FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) correct_predictions, total_predictions FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) latest

panels.description: Weighted accuracy: correct predictions / total reliable correlations. A prediction is correct when the market direction (above/below 50%) matches the actual weather outcome.

panels.title: Total Predictions Processed

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM market_correlations

panels.description: Total unique weather prediction markets that were successfully matched with real weather data. Deduplicated by condition_id.

panels.title: Active Alerts

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM anomaly_alerts WHERE is_resolved = FALSE

panels.description: Number of unresolved anomaly alerts in the system — accuracy drops, systematic bias detections, and arbitrage mispricing opportunities.

panels.title: Avg Prediction Error

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(avg_prediction_error)::numeric, 4) as value FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) avg_prediction_error FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) latest

panels.description: Mean absolute difference between the market YES price and the binary actual outcome (0 or 1). Range: 0.0 = perfect, 1.0 = completely wrong.

panels.title: Correlation Success Rate (KPI)

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM market_correlations) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NULL), 0), 1) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL

panels.description: Share of all fetched predictions that successfully matched with weather data and produced a valid correlation record.

panels.title: Closed Markets

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE closed = TRUE

panels.description: Markets where Manifold has officially resolved and declared a winner. Most reliable data source for accuracy measurement.

panels.title: Open Markets

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE closed = FALSE

panels.description: Markets still active without a resolved winner, correlated using historical weather data for past dates.

panels.title: Historical Weather Records

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE weather_type = 'historical'

panels.description: Correlations backed by Open-Meteo historical archive — verified weather observations for specific past dates.

panels.title: Unique Cities Tracked

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT location_name) as value FROM market_correlations

panels.description: Number of distinct cities and locations represented across all prediction markets in the dataset.

panels.title: Winner Known %

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE correlation_method = 'winner_known') * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) as value FROM market_correlations

panels.description: Percentage of markets where the winner is already declared by Manifold. Higher = more reliable accuracy signal.

panels.title: Accuracy by Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price>=0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price<0.5 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)*100, 1) as accuracy FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY accuracy DESC

panels.description: Accuracy gauge per market type. Green = market predictions align with actual weather. Red = systematic mismatch. Threshold: <60% red, <80% yellow, >=80% green.

panels.title: Avg Prediction Error by Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type, ROUND(AVG(prediction_error)::numeric, 3) as avg_error FROM market_correlations WHERE prediction_error IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY avg_error ASC

panels.description: Average prediction error per market type. Lower = better calibration. WIND and SNOW markets tend to have lower error as outcomes are more binary.

panels.title: Market Type Distribution

targets.rawSql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='RAIN' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "RAIN", SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='TEMPERATURE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "TEMPERATURE", SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='WIND' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "WIND", SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='SNOW' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "SNOW", SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='WEATHER' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "WEATHER", SUM(CASE WHEN market_type='SUNSHINE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "SUNSHINE" FROM market_correlations

panels.description: Distribution of all correlated markets by type. Shows which weather phenomena are most commonly traded on Manifold.

panels.title: Correct vs Incorrect Predictions

targets.rawSql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price>=0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price<0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "Correct", SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price<0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price>=0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "Incorrect" FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL

panels.description: Overall split between correct and incorrect predictions across all market types and locations.

panels.title: Best Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type as "Type", ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price>=0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price<0.5 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)*100,1) as "Accuracy %" FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 3

panels.description: Top 3 market types ranked by accuracy. Green = >85%, yellow = 70-85%, red = <70%.

panels.title: Worst Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type as "Type", ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price>=0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price<0.5 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(*),0)*100,1) as "Accuracy %" FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY 2 ASC LIMIT 3

panels.description: Bottom 3 market types ranked by accuracy — candidates for model improvement or exclusion.

panels.title: Avg Market Confidence

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(yes_price)*100,1) as value FROM market_correlations

panels.description: Average YES price across all markets. Low value (~38%) reflects that markets predominantly predict rare/negative weather events.

panels.title: Total Correct Predictions

targets.rawSql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome=1 AND yes_price>=0.5 OR actual_outcome=0 AND yes_price<0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL

panels.description: Absolute count of predictions where the market direction correctly matched the actual weather outcome.

panels.title: Predicted Probability vs Actual Win Rate by Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type, ROUND(AVG(yes_price)*100,1) as avg_price_pct, ROUND(AVG(actual_outcome)*100,1) as actual_win_rate FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY market_type

panels.description: Side-by-side comparison of what the market priced (blue) vs what actually happened (green). Gap indicates systematic over/under-pricing per market type.

panels.title: Scatter: Market Price vs Prediction Error
panels.description: Each dot = one market. X axis = market confidence (YES price), Y axis = prediction error. V-shape is expected: high-confidence markets (near 0% or 100%) should have low error.

targets.rawSql: SELECT yes_price, prediction_error, market_type, actual_outcome FROM market_correlations WHERE yes_price IS NOT NULL AND prediction_error IS NOT NULL ORDER BY market_type

panels.title: Heatmap: Win Rate by Market Type & Price Range
panels.description: Matrix of actual win rates by market type and price range. Green = market priced correctly, red = mispriced. Dash = no data in that bucket.

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN yes_price < 0.2 THEN actual_outcome::float END)*100) as "0-20%", ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN yes_price >= 0.2 AND yes_price < 0.4 THEN actual_outcome::float END)*100) as "20-40%", ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN yes_price >= 0.4 AND yes_price < 0.6 THEN actual_outcome::float END)*100) as "40-60%", ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN yes_price >= 0.6 AND yes_price < 0.8 THEN actual_outcome::float END)*100) as "60-80%", ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN yes_price >= 0.8 THEN actual_outcome::float END)*100) as "80-100%" FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY market_type

panels.title: Avg Prediction Error by YES Price Range

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN yes_price < 0.2 THEN '0.0-0.2' WHEN yes_price < 0.4 THEN '0.2-0.4' WHEN yes_price < 0.6 THEN '0.4-0.6' WHEN yes_price < 0.8 THEN '0.6-0.8' ELSE '0.8-1.0' END as price_range, ROUND(AVG(ABS(prediction_error))::numeric, 4) as avg_error FROM market_correlations WHERE yes_price IS NOT NULL AND prediction_error IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Average prediction error grouped by YES price range. High error in the 0.6-0.8 range indicates markets in that confidence zone are poorly calibrated.

panels.title: Market Calibration — Price vs Actual Win Rate
panels.description: Calibration chart: bars = actual win rate per price bucket, line = perfect calibration diagonal. Well-calibrated markets have bars matching the ideal line.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN yes_price < 0.1 THEN '0-10%' WHEN yes_price < 0.2 THEN '10-20%' WHEN yes_price < 0.3 THEN '20-30%' WHEN yes_price < 0.4 THEN '30-40%' WHEN yes_price < 0.5 THEN '40-50%' WHEN yes_price < 0.6 THEN '50-60%' WHEN yes_price < 0.7 THEN '60-70%' WHEN yes_price < 0.8 THEN '70-80%' WHEN yes_price < 0.9 THEN '80-90%' ELSE '90-100%' END as price_bucket, ROUND(AVG(actual_outcome)*100, 1) as actual_win_rate, ROUND(AVG(yes_price)*100, 1) as ideal_line, COUNT(*) as count FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL AND yes_price IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY MIN(yes_price)

panels.title: Accuracy by Location & Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT location_name as "Location", market_type as "Type", ROUND(accuracy_rate*100,1) as "Accuracy %", total_predictions as "Predictions", ROUND(avg_prediction_error::numeric,4) as "Avg Error", ROUND(bias_score::numeric,3) as "Bias" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) location_name, market_type, accuracy_rate, total_predictions, avg_prediction_error, bias_score FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) latest ORDER BY accuracy_rate ASC

panels.description: Per-location and market-type accuracy breakdown. Sorted by accuracy ascending to surface the worst-performing markets first.

panels.title: Accuracy by Market Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type as "Market Type", ROUND(AVG(accuracy_rate)*100,1) as "Accuracy %" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) market_type, accuracy_rate FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) latest GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.description: Accuracy aggregated by market type using weighted average. Useful for identifying which weather phenomena are hardest to predict.

panels.title: Bias Score by Location

targets.rawSql: SELECT location_name as "Location", ROUND(bias_score::numeric,3) as "Bias Score" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name) location_name, bias_score FROM market_accuracy_aggregates WHERE ABS(bias_score) > 0.1 ORDER BY location_name, updated_at DESC) latest ORDER BY bias_score ASC LIMIT 15

panels.description: Bias score per location: positive = market systematically over-predicts YES, negative = under-predicts. Values near 0 indicate unbiased markets.

panels.title: Closed Markets â€” Accuracy by Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT market_type as "Market Type", COUNT(*) as "Total", SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome = 1 AND yes_price >= 0.5 THEN 1 WHEN actual_outcome = 0 AND yes_price < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as "Correct", ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome = 1 AND yes_price >= 0.5 THEN 1 WHEN actual_outcome = 0 AND yes_price < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) as "Accuracy %", ROUND(AVG(prediction_error)::numeric, 4) as "Avg Error" FROM market_correlations WHERE closed = TRUE AND actual_outcome IS NOT NULL GROUP BY market_type ORDER BY 4 DESC

panels.description: Accuracy breakdown for closed markets (winner known) by type. The most reliable accuracy signal since outcomes are verified.

panels.title: Open Markets â€” Current Predictions

targets.rawSql: SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) location_name as "Location", market_type as "Type", ROUND(yes_price::numeric * 100, 1) as "YES Price %", actual_temp_c as "Temp Â°C", actual_precip_mm as "Precip mm", correlation_method as "Method", TO_CHAR(created_at, 'MM-DD HH24:MI') as "Time" FROM market_correlations WHERE closed = FALSE ORDER BY location_name, market_type, created_at DESC LIMIT 15

panels.description: Currently open markets with their YES price and weather correlation. These markets are still active and awaiting resolution.

panels.title: Top 10 Most Accurate Markets

targets.rawSql: SELECT location_name as "Location", market_type as "Type", ROUND(accuracy_rate*100,1) as "Accuracy %", total_predictions as "Predictions" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) location_name, market_type, accuracy_rate, total_predictions FROM market_accuracy_aggregates WHERE total_predictions >= 2 ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) latest ORDER BY accuracy_rate DESC, total_predictions DESC LIMIT 10

panels.description: Top 10 best-performing markets by accuracy rate. Minimum 1 prediction required.

panels.title: Producer Health Status
panels.description: Last heartbeat per producer. Minutes Ago = time since last run (green <5min, yellow <10min, red >10min = timeout alert triggered). Errors = actual processing failures (not filtered/skipped markets).

targets.rawSql: SELECT component as "Producer", status as "Status", ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - recorded_at))/60, 1) as "Minutes Ago", messages_processed as "Messages", error_count as "Errors", TO_CHAR(recorded_at, 'HH24:MI:SS') as "Last Seen" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (component) component, status, recorded_at, messages_processed, error_count FROM pipeline_health ORDER BY component, recorded_at DESC) latest ORDER BY component

panels.title: Producer Timeouts
panels.description: Number of active producer timeout alerts. Any value >0 means a producer has been silent for more than 10 minutes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM anomaly_alerts WHERE alert_type = 'producer_timeout' AND is_resolved = FALSE

panels.title: Last Pipeline Run
panels.description: Time since the most recent successful producer run across all components.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NOW() - MAX(recorded_at)))/60, 1) as value FROM pipeline_health WHERE status = 'healthy'

panels.title: Join Coverage Rate
panels.description: Percentage of prediction markets successfully matched with real weather data. Target: ≥95%. Below 70% triggers a critical alert.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE actual_outcome IS NOT NULL) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) as value FROM market_correlations

panels.title: Unmatched Predictions
panels.description: Number of prediction markets with no weather data match. These reduce join coverage. Investigate if this number grows unexpectedly.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) as value FROM market_correlations WHERE actual_outcome IS NULL

panels.title: Anomaly Detection Precision (est.)
panels.description: Estimated precision: alerts that remained active for >30 min / total alerts. Alerts auto-resolved within 30 min are likely false positives. Target: ≥80%.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(*) FILTER (
    WHERE is_resolved = FALSE
       OR (is_resolved = TRUE AND resolved_at > detected_at + INTERVAL '30 minutes')
  ) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)
, 1) as value
FROM anomaly_alerts
WHERE detected_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'

panels.title: Anomaly Detection Recall (est.)
panels.description: Estimated recall based on 2σ statistical threshold. By definition, a 2-sigma threshold captures ~95.4% of true anomalies in a normal distribution. Shows theoretical lower bound.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE alert_type IN ('accuracy_drop', 'bias_detected')) * 100.0
  / NULLIF(
    (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT location_name || market_type)
     FROM market_accuracy_aggregates
     WHERE accuracy_rate < 0.80 OR ABS(bias_score) > 0.30),
  0)
, 1) as value
FROM anomaly_alerts
WHERE is_resolved = FALSE
  AND alert_type IN ('accuracy_drop', 'bias_detected')

panels.title: Active Anomaly Alerts

targets.rawSql: SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type, alert_type) alert_type as "Type", severity as "Severity", location_name as "Location", market_type as "Market", ROUND(metric_value::numeric,3) as "Value", TO_CHAR(detected_at, 'HH24:MI:SS') as "Time" FROM anomaly_alerts WHERE is_resolved = FALSE ORDER BY location_name, market_type, alert_type, detected_at DESC LIMIT 20

panels.description: Active anomaly alerts from the detection job. Includes accuracy drops below the 7-day statistical baseline, bias spikes, and arbitrage opportunities.

panels.title: Alert Summary by Type

targets.rawSql: SELECT alert_type as "Alert Type", COUNT(*) as "Count" FROM anomaly_alerts GROUP BY alert_type ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.description: Count of alerts by type since the system started. Useful for understanding which anomaly type is most frequent.

panels.title: Correlation Methods Distribution

targets.rawSql: SELECT correlation_method as "Method", COUNT(*) as "Count" FROM market_correlations GROUP BY correlation_method ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.description: Distribution of correlation methods used. winner_known = Manifold resolved, historical_weather = Open-Meteo archive data.

panels.title: Correlation Methods â€” KPI Check

targets.rawSql: SELECT correlation_method as "Method", COUNT(*) as "Count", ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 1) as "Share %", ROUND(AVG(prediction_error)::numeric, 4) as "Avg Error" FROM market_correlations GROUP BY correlation_method ORDER BY 2 DESC

panels.description: Raw counts and share per correlation method. Validates that the pipeline is using the expected mix of data sources.

panels.title: Predictions by Month

targets.rawSql: SELECT TO_CHAR(observation_date, 'YYYY-MM') as "Month", COUNT(*) as "Count" FROM market_correlations WHERE observation_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1 ASC

panels.description: Number of prediction markets per calendar month based on observation date. Spike in recent months = new data from producers.

panels.title: Accuracy Trend by Month

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', observation_date) as time, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome = 1 AND yes_price >= 0.5 THEN 1 WHEN actual_outcome = 0 AND yes_price < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) as "Accuracy %" FROM market_correlations WHERE observation_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Rolling accuracy rate over time. Upward trend = model improving. Flat/downward = consistent market behavior or degradation.

panels.title: Market Accuracy by Year

targets.rawSql: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM observation_date)::int as "Year", COUNT(*) as "Predictions", ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN actual_outcome = 1 AND yes_price >= 0.5 THEN 1 WHEN actual_outcome = 0 AND yes_price < 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) as "Accuracy %", ROUND(AVG(prediction_error)::numeric, 4) as "Avg Error" FROM market_correlations WHERE observation_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1 DESC

panels.description: Accuracy and prediction error broken down by year. Useful for detecting if older markets behave differently from recent ones.

panels.title: Prediction Market Accuracy â€” World Map

targets.rawSql: SELECT cl.lat, cl.lon, cl.city_name, ROUND(AVG(a.accuracy_rate)::numeric * 100, 1) as accuracy_rate, SUM(a.total_predictions) as total_predictions FROM city_locations cl JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) location_name, accuracy_rate, total_predictions FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) a ON cl.city_name = a.location_name GROUP BY cl.lat, cl.lon, cl.city_name ORDER BY accuracy_rate

panels.description: World map showing accuracy rate per city. Circle size = number of predictions. Color = accuracy (green = high, red = low).

panels.title: Accuracy by City (Geographic Table)

targets.rawSql: SELECT location_name as "City", market_type as "Market Type", ROUND(accuracy_rate::numeric * 100, 1) as "accuracy_rate", ROUND(ABS(bias_score::numeric), 3) as "bias_score", ROUND(avg_prediction_error::numeric, 3) as "avg_error", total_predictions as "Predictions", ROUND(volume_weighted_accuracy::numeric * 100, 1) as "Vol.Weighted Acc %" FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (location_name, market_type) * FROM market_accuracy_aggregates ORDER BY location_name, market_type, updated_at DESC) t ORDER BY accuracy_rate ASC

panels.description: Full geographic breakdown: accuracy, bias, and prediction error per city and market type. Sortable by any column.
